reasoning: The 5-year forward inflation expectation rate recently spiked from a localized low of 2.05% in mid-April to 2.25% on April 24, before ticking slightly lower to 2.22% on April 25. The baseline 2.22% implies short-term downside risk as the series experiences localized mean reversion from this sharp jump. FRED's linear predictive model estimates a 36% chance of the value exceeding 2.22% in the near term, confirming possible downside momentum. Therefore, the short-term probability (May 4) of an increase is placed below 50% at 0.42. For longer horizons, the series historically spent most of 2024 nearer to 2.30%, but forecasting longer-term macroeconomic shifts introduces substantial uncertainty. Thus, adhering to the instruction, probabilities rise progressively toward 0.50 as the forecast horizon widens, reflecting standard increasing term uncertainty.
